steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes: one of many nonpresidents ______ on US currency. The phrase “one of many [noun] to [verb]” is a standard English construction using an infinitive to indicate membership in a group defined by that action. The blank needs the infinitive form of “appear.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'to appear': Fits the pattern “one of many nonpresidents to appear on US currency” — a reduced relative clause meaning “who have appeared.” ✓
  • Choice B — 'appears': A finite present-tense verb creates a grammatically broken sentence: “one of many nonpresidents appears on US currency” conflicts with “making...one of many.” ✗
  • Choice C — 'did appear': A finite past-tense verb, same structural problem as B. ✗
  • Choice D — 'had appeared': Past perfect finite verb — same structural problem; does not fit the infinitive construction required here. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
